summary: Devlin et al. introduce BERT, a deep bidirectional Transformer pre-trained on unlabeled text via masked language modelling and next-sentence prediction, which sets new state-of-the-art results on eleven NLP benchmarks including GLUE (80.5%), SQuAD v1.1 F1 (93.2), and SQuAD v2.0 F1 (83.1) by fine-tuning with a single output layer.

blocks.content: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

source_span.quote: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding

blocks.content: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers.

source_span.quote: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers.

blocks.content: Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers.

source_span.quote: Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers.

blocks.content: As a result, the pre-trained BERT model can be fine-tuned with just one additional output layer to create state-of-the-art models for a wide range of tasks, such as question answering and language inference, without substantial task-specific architecture modifications.

source_span.quote: As a result, the pre-trained BERT model can be fine-tuned with just one additional output layer to create state-of-the-art models for a wide range of tasks, such as question answering and language inference, without substantial task-specific architecture modifications.

blocks.content: It obtains new state-of-the-art results on eleven natural language processing tasks, including pushing the GLUE score to 80.5% (7.7% point absolute improvement), MultiNLI accuracy to 86.7% (4.6% absolute improvement), SQuAD v1.1 question answering Test F1 to 93.2 (1.5 point absolute improvement) and SQuAD v2.0 Test F1 to 83.1 (5.1 point absolute improvement).

source_span.quote: It obtains new state-of-the-art results on eleven natural language processing tasks, including pushing the GLUE score to 80.5% (7.7% point absolute improvement), MultiNLI accuracy to 86.7% (4.6% absolute improvement), SQuAD v1.1 question answering Test F1 to 93.2 (1.5 point absolute improvement) and SQuAD v2.0 Test F1 to 83.1 (5.1 point absolute improvement).

blocks.content: BERT is conceptually simple and empirically powerful.

source_span.quote: BERT is conceptually simple and empirically powerful.
